content:
# Luke Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 95-97

---

**1.** And it came to pass afterward [that] he was going through according to city and village, preaching and proclaiming-good-news [of] the kingdom of God, and the twelve [were] with him,

**2.** and certain women who had been healed from evil spirits and infirmities: Mary, the [one] called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out,

**3.** and Joanna, wife of Chuza, steward of Herod, and Susanna, and many others, who were ministering to them out of the [things] belonging to them.

**4.** And a great crowd having gathered together, and those from each city journeying to him, he spoke through a parable:

**5.** "The [one] sowing went out to sow his seed. And in the [course of] his sowing, some fell beside the way, and it was trampled down, and the birds of the heaven devoured it.

**6.** And other fell upon the rock, and having sprung up, it withered on account of not having moisture.

**7.** And other fell in [the] midst of the thorns, and the thorns having sprung up together choked it.

**8.** And other fell into the good earth, and having sprung up, it produced fruit a hundredfold." Saying these [things], he was calling out, "The [one] having ears to hear, let him hear."

**9.** And his disciples were questioning him, [asking] what this parable might be.

**10.** And he said, "To you it has been given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of God, but to the rest in parables, so that seeing they might not see, and hearing they might not understand.

**11.** And this is the parable: The seed is the word of God.

**12.** And those beside the way are the [ones] having heard; then the devil comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they might not, having believed, be saved.

**13.** And those upon the rock [are those] who, whenever they hear, receive the word with joy; and these have no root, who for a time believe, and in [a] time of testing fall away.

**14.** And that having fallen into the thorns, these are the [ones] having heard, and under cares and riches and pleasures of life, going along, they are choked and do not bring [fruit] to completion.

**15.** And that in the good earth, these are those who, having heard the word in a noble and good heart, hold [it] fast and bear fruit in patient endurance.

**16.** And no one, having lit a lamp, covers it with a vessel or places [it] under a bed, but places [it] upon a lampstand, so that those entering in might see the light.

**17.** For there is nothing hidden that shall not become manifest, nor secret that shall not be known and come into [the] open.

**18.** See then how you hear; for whoever has, [more] shall be given to him, and whoever does not have, even what he thinks he has shall be taken from him."

**19.** And his mother and his brothers came to him, and they were not able to reach him on account of the crowd.

**20.** And it was reported to him, "Your mother and your brothers have been standing outside, wanting to see you."

**21.** But he, having answered, said to them, "My mother and my brothers are these — the [ones] hearing the word of God and doing [it]."

**22.** And it came to pass on one of the days [that] he himself embarked into a boat, and his disciples, and he said to them, "Let us cross over to the other side of the lake." And they set sail.

**23.** And [as] they were sailing, he fell asleep. And a storm of wind came down upon the lake, and they were being swamped and were in danger.

**24.** And having come to [him], they roused him, saying, "Master, master, we are perishing!" And he, having risen, rebuked the wind and the raging of the water; and they ceased, and [a] calm came.

**25.** And he said to them, "Where [is] your faith?" And having been afraid, they marveled, saying to one another, "Who then is this, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?"

**26.** And they sailed down to the region of the Gerasenes, which is opposite Galilee.

**27.** And [as] he went out upon the land, a certain man from the city met him, having demons, and for a considerable time he had not put on a garment, and he was not abiding in a house but in the tombs.

**28.** And having seen Jesus, [and] having cried out, he fell before him and with a great voice said, "What [is there] to me and to you, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me."

**29.** For he was commanding the unclean spirit to come out from the man. For many times it had seized him, and he was being bound with chains and fetters, being guarded, and breaking apart the bonds, he was being driven by the demon into the desolate [places].

**30.** And Jesus questioned him, "What is your name?" And he said, "Legion," because many demons had entered into him.

**31.** And they were imploring him that he would not command them to go away into the abyss.

**32.** And there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain. And they implored him that he would permit them to enter into those. And he permitted them.

**33.** And the demons, having gone out from the man, entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep [bank] into the lake and was drowned.

**34.** And the [ones] feeding [them], having seen what had happened, fled and reported [it] into the city and into the countryside.

**35.** And they went out to see what had happened, and they came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting, clothed and being sound in mind, at the feet of Jesus. And they were afraid.

**36.** And those having seen [it] reported to them how the [one] having been demon-possessed was saved.

**37.** And the whole multitude of the surrounding region of the Gerasenes asked him to go away from them, because they were being held by a great fear. And he, having embarked into a boat, returned.

**38.** And the man from whom the demons had gone out was begging [to be] with him. But he sent him away, saying,

**39.** "Return to your house and declare what things God has done for you." And he went away through the whole city, proclaiming what things Jesus had done for him.

**40.** And in the returning of Jesus, the crowd welcomed him, for they were all expecting him.

**41.** And behold, a man came, whose name [was] Jairus, and this [man] was a ruler of the synagogue. And having fallen at the feet of Jesus, he was imploring him to come into his house,

**42.** because he had an only-born daughter, about twelve years [of age], and she was dying. And in his going, the crowds were pressing upon him.

**43.** And a woman, being in a flow of blood from twelve years, who, having spent her whole livelihood on physicians, was not able to be healed by anyone,

**44.** having come up behind, touched the fringe of his garment, and immediately the flow of her blood stopped.

**45.** And Jesus said, "Who [is] the [one] having touched me?" And [when] all were denying [it], Peter said, "Master, the crowds are pressing on you and crushing [you]."

**46.** But Jesus said, "Someone touched me, for I knew power having gone out from me."

**47.** And the woman, having seen that she had not escaped notice, came trembling, and having fallen before him, declared before all the people for what reason she touched him, and how she was healed immediately.

**48.** And he said to her, "Daughter, your faith has saved you; go in peace."

**49.** [While] he [was] still speaking, someone comes from the [household of the] ruler of the synagogue, saying, "Your daughter has died; no longer trouble the teacher."

**50.** But Jesus, having heard, answered him, "Do not fear; only believe, and she shall be saved."

**51.** And having come into the house, he did not allow anyone to enter with him except Peter and John and James, and the father of the child and the mother.

**52.** And all were weeping and mourning her. But he said, "Do not weep, for she did not die, but she sleeps."

**53.** And they were laughing at him, knowing that she died.

**54.** But he, having grasped her hand, called out, saying, "Child, arise."

**55.** And her spirit returned, and she rose up immediately. And he directed [something] to be given to her to eat.

**56.** And her parents were astonished. But he charged them to tell no one what had happened.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **8:3 — "to them" (αὐτοῖς):** Sinaiticus reads αὐτοῖς ("to them"), whereas many later manuscripts read αὐτῷ ("to him"), directing the women's service to Jesus alone. The plural reading in Sinaiticus may include the twelve disciples as recipients.

2. **8:26 — "Gerasenes" (Γερασηνῶν):** Sinaiticus reads Γερασηνῶν ("Gerasenes"). Other manuscript traditions vary between Γαδαρηνῶν ("Gadarenes") and Γεργεσηνῶν ("Gergesenes"). The geographic identification differs across witnesses.

3. **8:27 — "having demons":** Sinaiticus uses the plural form here, consistent with the narrative context that reveals the name "Legion" (v. 30). Some witnesses use the singular.

4. **8:37 — "Gerasenes":** Again Sinaiticus reads Γερασηνῶν, consistent with v. 26. The same variant tradition applies.

5. **8:43 — Inclusion of the physician clause:** Sinaiticus includes the phrase about the woman having spent her whole livelihood on physicians. Some Western witnesses (notably Codex Bezae) omit this clause, possibly through harmonization with Mark 5:26 or scribal sensitivity.

6. **8:45 — "Peter said":** Sinaiticus attributes the response to Peter specifically. Some manuscripts add "and those with him," reflecting a variant tradition expanding the speakers.

7. **8:46 — "I knew" (ἔγνων):** Sinaiticus reads ἔγνων (aorist "I knew/perceived"), whereas the Byzantine tradition typically reads ἐγὼ γὰρ ἔγνων ("for I myself knew"). The Sinaiticus reading is more terse.

8. **8:48 — "Daughter, your faith has saved you":** Sinaiticus preserves σέσωκέν σε (perfect tense: "has saved you"). The verb σῴζω encompasses both physical healing and spiritual salvation — the literal translation retains this ambiguity.

9. **8:49 — "someone comes" (ἔρχεταί τις):** Sinaiticus uses the historical present tense ἔρχεται ("comes") rather than the aorist ἦλθεν ("came"), creating a vivid, immediate narrative effect. Preserved as "comes" in the translation.

10. **8:51 — Disciple order:** Sinaiticus reads "Peter and John and James," placing John before James. This differs from the typical Synoptic order "Peter and James and John" found in many manuscripts, and reflects a Lukan preference also seen elsewhere in Luke-Acts.

11. **8:54 — "Child, arise":** Sinaiticus reads ἡ παῖς ἔγειρε. The Aramaic Talitha koum found in Mark 5:41 is not present in Luke's Greek text. Some manuscripts add "arise" (ἔγειρε) without the article.

12. **8:4 — "through a parable" (διὰ παραβολῆς):** Sinaiticus uses the singular παραβολῆς ("a parable"), where some later witnesses read the plural παραβολῶν ("parables").

13. **8:25 — "having been afraid" (φοβηθέντες):** Sinaiticus uses the aorist passive participle, emphasizing fear as the initial response before marvel. The word order and phrasing vary slightly across witnesses.
